rules.description: Important transferable elements, including decorative and asset roots, must have a stable identity: a stable layer name or an identity-bearing fallback tag value.
rules.fix: Use an English kebab-case layer name when Figma metadata provides the type. Boolean `[decor]` and `[asset]` are visual-intent/policy flags and are not enough by themselves to identify a poorly named layer.

rules.description: Two elements inside the same breakpoint/view scope must not share one stable identity.
rules.fix: Rename identities or add instance indexes such as `product-card-1`, `product-card-2`.

rules.description: The same identity must not resolve to different logical element types across breakpoints.
rules.fix: Keep the Figma node/component type stable or model a separate entity.

rules.description: Optional BRIDGE identity values must describe logical elements, not responsive breakpoints. A value such as `[control=button-reviews-box-375]` inside a `[bp=375]` root is invalid when the suffix matches the current breakpoint.
rules.fix: Remove the breakpoint suffix from the optional id. For ordinary buttons/controls, remove the optional id completely and use `Отзывы мобилка [action=modal:marketplaces-modal]`.

rules.description: Repeated list/grid items need stable instance identities.
rules.fix: Use indexed identities such as `feature-card-1`, `feature-card-2`.

rules.description: One layer must not declare multiple competing identity tags such as `[section=...]` and `[modal=...]`.
rules.fix: Keep the one tag that represents the layer's logical identity. Express behavior and visual intent with non-identity tags such as `[href=...]`, `[action=...]`, `[decor]`, or `[asset]`.

rules.description: Boolean `[decor]` and `[asset]` are valid without values. If a fallback value is present in `[decor=...]` or `[asset=...]`, it must be English kebab-case.
rules.fix: Prefer a stable layer identity plus boolean flags, for example `sneg [decor] [asset]`. Use value form only for poor/default layer names, for example `Frame 182 [decor=snow-bg]`.

rules.description: Authored identity-like tag values, anchors, and views should use stable English kebab-case so validators and adapters can compare them deterministically.
rules.fix: Replace spaces, localized words, uppercase characters, and punctuation with a stable English kebab-case value such as `product-card-1`, `contact-modal`, `faq`, or `not-found`.

rules.description: `[decor]` and `[asset]` are visual-intent/policy flags and must not be counted as identity tags or as multiple identities when they appear together.
rules.fix: Resolve identity from the stable layer name such as `sneg`; keep `[decor]` and `[asset]` as flags. Use `[decor=...]` or `[asset=...]` only as fallback for poor/default layer names.

rules.description: A layer must not repeat the same BRIDGE tag key.
rules.fix: Keep one canonical value for the key. If two values describe different concerns, model them with the appropriate distinct tags or split the logical entities.

rules.description: Tags derived from native Figma metadata, including `[text]`, `[image]`, `[icon]`, `[container]`, `[layout]`, `[abs]`, and `[component]`, must not be authored in layer names.
rules.fix: Remove the metadata tag and express the property through the native Figma node type, component source, layout mode, positioning, fills, or export settings.

rules.description: Section layer names should not repeat the role with prefixes such as `Section /` or `Секция /`.
rules.fix: Keep the human label concise. Resolve the section from the `Page Sections` source component when possible, or use `[section=...]` only for frame sections, generic components, or explicit overrides.

rules.description: A section key should describe the reusable section component, not copy the page-specific heading.
rules.fix: Use the layer name for the contextual heading. Resolve the stable section key from `Page Sections` or use `[section=...]` only when an explicit fallback/override is needed.

rules.description: A page-level section component instance should come from the `Page Sections` library page, or the layer should declare an explicit `[section=...]` fallback.
rules.fix: Move the source component to `Page Sections`, register it as a section component, or add `[section=...]` only when the layer is a frame section, a generic component, or an explicit override.

rules.description: Instances of components from `Page Sections` should not repeat `[section=...]` on the page layer.
rules.fix: Remove the redundant page-layer `[section=...]`; resolve section id from the source component or component set name.

rules.description: Components from `UI Kit` are ordinary interface components and should not be inferred as page sections.
rules.fix: Use a component from `Page Sections` for a page-level section, or add `[section=...]` only for a deliberate frame section or override.

rules.description: Default names such as `Frame 53` or `Group 271` usually hide missing structure or intent.
rules.fix: Rename the layer with a stable English kebab-case identity; add BRIDGE intent tags only where needed.

rules.description: Every layer must be classifiable from Figma metadata or BRIDGE intent tags.
rules.fix: Fix the Figma structure/metadata first; add BRIDGE tags only for product or transfer intent such as `[section=...]`, `[href=...]`, `[link]`, `[action=...]`, `[control]`, `[field=...]`, `[decor]`, `[asset]`, `[modal=...]`, or `[state=...]`.

rules.description: Visually detached or overlapping elements must have clear intent.
rules.fix: Use Figma positioning for geometry and add intent only when needed: `[decor]`, `[asset]`, `[modal=...]`, `[state=...]`, or `[bridge-exception=...] [reason=...]`.

rules.description: A one-child wrapper without a structural reason is likely unnecessary noise.
rules.fix: Remove the wrapper or make its Figma purpose clear through structure, naming, clipping, shared surface, target scope, or asset grouping.

rules.description: Deep wrapper chains often make responsive mapping fragile.
rules.fix: Flatten structure or ensure each wrapper has a real Figma purpose.

rules.description: Overlapping bounds require clear overlay/decor/asset intent or an explicit exception.
rules.fix: Declare `[decor]`, `[asset]`, a target tag, or a BRIDGE exception reason. Positioning comes from Figma metadata.

rules.description: Layer order is visually important but not declared as overlay/stacking intent.
rules.fix: Declare overlay scope or stacking behavior.

rules.description: Responsive root frames must declare `[bp=...]`.
rules.fix: Add explicit breakpoint tags to root frames.

rules.description: An important identity exists on one breakpoint but is missing on another required breakpoint.
rules.fix: Keep the same identity in the same parent and change breakpoint visibility instead of deleting it, or model a separate state/variant/collection rule/structural exception.

rules.description: Responsive breakpoint roots for the same view do not preserve the same logical element tree/cardinality.
rules.fix: Keep the same transferable identities across breakpoints, or model the difference as a collection rule, state, variant, or structural exception.

rules.description: A transferable identity changes parent identity across breakpoints.
rules.fix: Preserve parent-child topology, use shared wrappers, or declare an explicit structural exception.

rules.description: Sibling order changed across breakpoints without an order strategy.
rules.fix: Preserve semantic order or declare an order strategy.

rules.description: The same responsive identity has different visual-intent/policy tags across breakpoints, for example `sneg [decor] [asset]` on desktop and plain `sneg` on mobile.
rules.fix: Keep visual-intent flags stable across breakpoints, or model the difference as an explicit state, variant, target-specific rule, or BRIDGE exception.

rules.description: The same product text identity has different content across breakpoints. Text inside decorative or root asset visuals is excluded from product-content drift checks.
rules.fix: Keep responsive product text identical. Model locale, experiment, or product variants outside breakpoint variants. For decorative or root asset visuals, preserve the root identity and visual-intent tags instead of comparing inner text as product content.

rules.description: Text inside `[decor]` layers or root `[asset]` visuals is not product content and should not be reported as product-content drift.
rules.fix: Skip inner text product-drift comparison for decorative/root asset visuals, but still validate root identity coverage and visual-intent stability across breakpoints.

rules.description:
Dynamic text must not rely on forced line breaks or
to fit the layout.
rules.fix: Let text wrap by its container using hug/min-height, max-width, and explicit overflow/truncation rules. Use `[bridge-exception=manual-line-break] [reason=...]` only for semantic or approved brand-lockup breaks.

rules.description: A manual line break exception must explain why the break is semantic or brand-required.
rules.fix: Pair `[bridge-exception=manual-line-break]` with `[reason=...]`, for example `[reason=brand-lockup]` or `[reason=postal-address-format]`.

rules.description: Strict content such as price, legal copy, or CTA changed across breakpoints.
rules.fix: Synchronize strict content or document an approved variant.

rules.description: Hidden keyed layers may contain alternative source-of-truth content.
rules.fix: Move alternatives into declared states/variants or archive them.

rules.description: A visually clickable element has no known interaction marker: no `[href=...]`, `[action=...]`, `[link]`, or `[control]`.
rules.fix: Add `[href=...]` for known navigation, `[action=...]` for known non-navigation behavior, `[link]` for an unknown draft destination, or `[control]` for an unknown draft action.

rules.description: Boolean `[control]` or advanced `[control=...]` without `[action=...]` is a valid draft marker for an unknown action, not a syntax error. Final handoff should resolve it.
rules.fix: If the action is known, replace the draft marker with `[action=modal:...]`, `[action=state:...]`, `[action=submit:...]`, `[action=reset:...]`, or `[action=none]`. Keep `[control=...]` only as an optional advanced id when a stable machine id is needed.

rules.description: Boolean `[link]` or advanced `[link=...]` without `[href=...]` is a valid draft marker for an unknown destination, not a syntax error. Final handoff should resolve it.
rules.fix: If the destination is known, use `[href=...]`. Keep `[link=...]` only as an optional advanced id when a stable machine id is needed. Do not use `[href=#]` as a placeholder.

rules.description: Links must not declare a second destination such as `[to=...]` or `[action=link:...]` alongside `[href=...]`.
rules.fix: Keep only `[href=...]` for navigation destination. `[href=...]` is already enough to classify the layer as a link.

rules.description: `[href=#]` is not a valid unknown href placeholder. `#faq` is a real same-page anchor; `#` alone does not declare a destination.
rules.fix: Use `[link]` while the destination is unknown, or replace `#` with a real href such as `[href=#faq]` or `[href=/contacts]`.

rules.description: Advanced optional ids in `[link=...]` and `[control=...]` must be English kebab-case and must not include breakpoint names or width suffixes when a value is present.
rules.fix: Prefer no optional id for ordinary links/buttons: use `[href=...]`, `[link]`, `[action=...]`, or `[control]`. If an id is required, use a breakpoint-neutral kebab-case value.

rules.description: An `[href=...]` value must be a supported real destination: an internal path, same-page anchor, `http`/`https` URL, `mailto:` URL, or `tel:` URL.
rules.fix: Correct the destination syntax. If the destination is not known yet, remove `[href=...]` and use the boolean draft marker `[link]`.

rules.description: An `[action=...]` value must use a supported action grammar: `modal:target-id`, `state:target-id`, `submit:form-id`, `reset:target-id`, or `none`.
rules.fix: Correct the action value and use a breakpoint-neutral kebab-case target id. If the action is not known yet, remove `[action=...]` and use the boolean draft marker `[control]`.

rules.description: One control must not declare more than one `[action=...]` value.
rules.fix: Keep one canonical action. Model alternative behavior as an explicit state, variant, or separate control.

rules.description: A modal action must point to an existing modal target.
rules.fix: Create `[modal=target-id]` target or change the action.

rules.description: A state action must point to an existing state target.
rules.fix: Create `[state=target-id]` target or change the action.

rules.description: A submit action must point to an existing form target in the same page contract.
rules.fix: Create `[form=target-id]` or change `[action=submit:...]` to the correct form id.

rules.description: A reset action should point to an existing state, form, or other declared reset target in the same page contract.
rules.fix: Create the declared target or change `[action=reset:...]` to the identity that owns the resettable state.

rules.description: Modal targets need close behavior.
rules.fix: Add close button/action and define backdrop/escape behavior.

rules.description: Form fields need labels or accessible names.
rules.fix: Add label text and field identity.

rules.description: Forms need a submit action or declared endpoint/handler.
rules.fix: Add `[action=submit:...]` to the submit control or form.

rules.description: Interactive or dynamic components often need empty, loading, error, disabled, hover, or focus states.
rules.fix: Add required states or declare why they are not needed.

rules.description: Fixed height must be intentional.
rules.fix: Use hug/min-height or add `[height=fixed] [reason=...]`.

rules.description: Clipped surfaces need explicit overflow policy.
rules.fix: Add `[overflow=hidden]`, `[overflow=visible]`, `[overflow=scroll]`, or `[overflow=truncate]`.

rules.description: Text appears likely to clip due to fixed height or insufficient bounds.
rules.fix: Use hug height, min-height, or truncation policy.

rules.description: Text-like content should not be exported as a raster asset without reason.
rules.fix: Use native text or add asset exception reason.

rules.description: Responsive images need a crop/focal strategy.
rules.fix: Declare focal point, fit mode, or crop policy.

rules.description: Video/Lottie/complex media should define fallback behavior.
rules.fix: Add poster, fallback asset, controls, and autoplay policy.

rules.description: Color, typography, radius, or spacing values appear detached from tokens.
rules.fix: Use design tokens or declare an exception.

rules.description: Interactive target is smaller than recommended touch size.
rules.fix: Increase hit area or declare target-specific exception.

rules.description: Text/background contrast may be insufficient.
rules.fix: Adjust colors or document intentional exception.

rules.description: A `[decor]` layer should not be exposed as product content in the accessibility tree.
rules.fix: Treat the transferred decorative layer as `aria-hidden` or otherwise exclude it from accessibility/content semantics.

rules.description: Page instances should not duplicate Figma component source with `[component=...]` tags.
rules.fix: Remove `[component=...]` from page instance names and rely on Figma/UI Kit metadata.

rules.description: UI Kit component source is missing required states for its component family.
rules.fix: Add states to the UI Kit component set instead of drawing them on a page.

rules.description: A page root without `[route=...]` or `[route-pattern=...]` is a Draft TODO when the production route is not known yet, not a hard error.
rules.fix: Do not invent fake routes. Add `[route=/path]` or `[route-pattern=/path/:param]` only when the real production route is known.

rules.description: Page preflight requires at least one root frame with a stable `[page=...]` identity.
rules.fix: Add `[page=page-id]` to every responsive root frame that belongs to the page, then run preflight for that page identity.

rules.description: Every page identity should declare a `view=default` root for its normal product state.
rules.fix: Add `[view=default]` to the normal page roots, or create the default view before modeling empty, loading, error, or permission-specific views.

rules.description: When `[route=...]` or `[route-pattern=...]` is present, it must be a real production URL/path or route template, not a fake placeholder.
rules.fix: Remove the route while unknown, or replace it with the real production route such as `[route=/contacts]` or `[route-pattern=/catalog/:slug]`.

rules.description: Two concrete pages must not declare the same route unless intentionally modeled as views of one page.
rules.fix: Use one `[page=...]` with multiple `[view=...]` states, or change the duplicate route.

rules.description: Internal href points to a route that is not declared in the known route contract.
rules.fix: Add a page with the matching real `[route=...]` when known, correct the href, or leave route resolution as a draft TODO if page routes are not known yet.

rules.description: Internal href points to an anchor that is not declared on the target page.
rules.fix: Add `[anchor=...]` to the target section or correct the href fragment.

rules.description: Views or breakpoints of one page identity must not use different known production routes.
rules.fix: Keep one known `[route=...]` across all `[view=...]` and `[bp=...]` variants of the page. If the route is unknown, omit it consistently until it is known.

rules.description: Product content changed across breakpoints without a non-responsive variation axis. Decorative and root asset text is not product content drift.
rules.fix: Keep breakpoint product content identical, or model the change with `[view=...]`, `[locale=...]`, `[experiment=...]`, or another explicit axis. Preserve decorative/asset root identities and tags across breakpoints.

rules.description: Theme variants must not change text content.
rules.fix: Keep content identical across themes; theme should change visual tokens only.

rules.description: One variation axis appears to hide a different reason for variation.
rules.fix: Use the correct axis: breakpoint for layout, view for data state, locale for translation, experiment for A/B, role-view for permissions.

rules.description: The selected target adapter does not support a declared feature.
rules.fix: Change the design, add asset fallback, or choose another adapter.
